title.b: 6930 Print surface temperature had to be adapted due to environment temperature and humidity

abstract.p: 6930 will be reported when the dew point is narrowing the platen temperature. When the environmental temperature and humidity is increasing, condensation might appear on the platen and curing lamps since the chiller set-point is typically having a lower temperature (e.g. 24 degrees Celsius). Dew water droplets must be prevented.

Solution is implemented within the printer to increase the chiller set-point. When this happens, the 6930 is reported. It is only an indication that mostly the room temperature in combination with the humidity is too high.
